explanations_examples: Basic confirmation. Example: כן, אני בא. — Yes, I’m coming.
Basic negation. Example: לא, תודה. — No, thanks.
Used everywhere, formal and informal. Example: תודה על העזרה. — Thanks for the help.
Means both “please” and “you’re welcome.” Example: בבקשה תעזור לי. — Please help me.
Used to apologize or get attention. Example: סליחה, איפה השירותים? — Excuse me, where are the bathroom?
Basic question word. Example: מה זה? — What is this?
Basic question word. Example: מי זה? — Who is that?
Basic question word. Example: איפה אתה גר? — Where do you live?
Basic question word. Example: מתי ניפגש? — When will we meet?
Basic question word. Example: למה אתה עייף? — Why are you tired?
Basic question word. Example: איך מגיעים לשם? — How do you get there?
First-person singular. Example: אני רעב. — I’m hungry.
Masculine singular “you.” Example: אתה בבית? — Are you at home?
Feminine singular “you.” Example: את מוכנה? — Are you ready?
Third-person masculine. Example: הוא חבר שלי. — He is my friend.
Third-person feminine. Example: היא עובדת פה. — She works here.
First-person plural. Example: אנחנו הולכים לים. — We’re going to the beach.
Very common in speech. Example: אני יוצא עכשיו. — I’m leaving now.
Basic time word. Example: היום חם מאוד. — Today is very hot.
Basic time word. Example: מחר יש לי פגישה. — Tomorrow I have a meeting.
Basic time word. Example: אתמול הייתי בתל אביב. — Yesterday I was in Tel Aviv.
Very common adjective. Example: זה רעיון טוב. — That’s a good idea.
Opposite of טוב. Example: זה לא רע. — That’s not bad.
Masculine form. Feminine: גדולה. Example: הבית גדול. — The house is big.
Masculine form. Feminine: קטנה. Example: החדר קטן. — The room is small.
For weather, food, or temperature. Example: הקפה חם. — The coffee is hot.
For weather, food, or temperature. Example: בחוץ קר. — It’s cold outside.
Very useful for food. Example: האוכל טעים. — The food is tasty.
Plural-looking noun but usually used as singular meaning. Example: אני רוצה מים. — I want water.
Also can mean “eating” or “I eat” depending on context. Example: יש פה אוכל טוב. — There’s good food here.
Basic noun. Example: אני בבית. — I’m at home.
Useful for directions. Example: הרחוב הזה שקט. — This street is quiet.
Basic everyday word. Example: אין לי כסף מזומן. — I don’t have cash.
Basic abstract noun. Example: אין לי זמן עכשיו. — I don’t have time now.
Can mean the workplace or work in general. Example: אני בדרך לעבודה. — I’m on the way to work.
Masculine friend; feminine: חברה. Also can mean boyfriend. Example: הוא חבר טוב. — He is a good friend.
Feminine friend, girlfriend, or company depending on context. Example: היא חברה שלי. — She is my friend / girlfriend.
Very common slang from Arabic, used constantly. Example: יאללה, בוא נלך. — Come on, let’s go.
Very common casual word meaning fine, okay, cool. Example: סבבה, נדבר מחר. — Cool, we’ll talk tomorrow.
Casual positive word. Example: אחלה רעיון. — Great idea.
Flexible slang word; can show surprise, agreement, or hesitation. Example: וואלה, לא ידעתי. — Really, I didn’t know.
Casual word for something cool or interesting. Example: זה ממש מגניב. — That’s really cool.
Slang for something disappointing. Example: איזה באסה, הפסדנו. — What a bummer, we lost.
Used when something is unfortunate. Example: חבל שלא באת. — Too bad you didn’t come.
Very common. Example: היה ממש כיף. — It was really fun.
Informal address, mostly masculine-coded but widely heard. Example: אחי, מה קורה? — Bro, what’s up?
Everyday greeting. Example: היי, מה קורה? — Hey, what’s up?
Common answer to “what’s up?” Example: הכל טוב, תודה. — All good, thanks.
Slang meaning something is minor/easy. Example: זה בקטנה, אני אטפל בזה. — No big deal, I’ll handle it.
Slang meaning someone is easygoing or willing. Example: אתה זורם לקפה? — Are you down for coffee?
Slang; literally “digging.” Example: הוא קצת חופר. — He talks too much / he’s being annoying.
